revision.comment: 「白鳥古墳」、曖昧さ回避として立てました。広島方面の方よろしく(笑)
山口県のを失念してました・・・
revision.text:
'''白鳥古墳'''は、日本の古墳の名称。
*[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] - 愛知県名古屋市 *[[白鳥古墳 (東広島市)]] - 広島県東広島市 *[[白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] - 山口県熊毛郡平生町 [[Category:日本の古墳|しらとりこふん]] {{aimai}}

'''白鳥古墳'''は、日本の古墳の名称。
*[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] - 愛知県名古屋市 *[[白鳥古墳 (東広島市)]] - 広島県東広島市 *[[白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] - 山口県熊毛郡平生町
:各地の[[白鳥塚古墳]]については左記の項目を参照。
[[Category:日本の古墳|しらとりこふん]] {{aimai}}

'''白鳥古墳'''（しらとりこふん、しろとりこふん）は、日本の古墳の名称。
* [[白鳥古墳群]] - 愛知県名古屋市守山区。しらとりこふんぐん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] - 愛知県名古屋市熱田区。しろとりこふん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (東広島市)]] - 広島県東広島市。しらとりこふん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] - 山口県熊毛郡平生町。しらとりこふん。
== 関連項目 == * [[白鳥塚古墳]]
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の古墳|しらとりこふん]]

revision.text:
'''白鳥古墳'''（しらとりこふん、しろとりこふん）は、日本の古墳の名称。
* [[白鳥古墳群]] - 愛知県名古屋市守山区。しらとりこふんぐん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] - 愛知県名古屋市熱田区。しろとりこふん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (東広島市)]] - 広島県東広島市。しらとりこふん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] - 山口県熊毛郡平生町。しらとりこふん。
== 関連項目 == * [[白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)]]
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の古墳|しらとりこふん]]

The mirrors are predominantly exca..."
revision.text: == Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}}|| Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|ja|会津若松市}} || || 4th century || Among the oldest in Tohoku, designated as a National Historic Site in 1972. |- ! colspan=5 | Kanto Region |- | {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Burial Mound|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}} || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Two Four-God Four-Beast Mirrors excavated. |- | {{ill|Tamonoko Burial Mound|ja|頼母子古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}} || Unknown (Keyhole-shaped mound is likely) || Mid-4th century || Two triangular-rimmed mirrors (one location unknown). |- | {{ill|Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound|ja|北山茶臼山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Tomioka|ja|富岡市}} || Round mound (possible keyhole shape) || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|ja|蟹沢古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}} || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}} || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|ja|真土大塚山古墳}} (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|ja|平塚市}}, Nishimatoda || || revision.comment: mass edit 1 before doing entries after Nishi Tomezuka Burial Mound
revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- ! colspan=5| |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | {{ill|Shiratori Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白鳥古墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en}}[[ja:東広島市]], Takaya Town || || || |- | {{ill|Shiozaki Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:潮崎山古墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuyama|en}}[[ja:福山市]], Shinichi Town || || || |- | {{ill|Nakakoda No. 1 Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:中小田1号墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Hiroshima|en}}[[ja:広島市]], Higashi Ward || || || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound |- | {{ill|Kambara Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神原神社古墳]] || Shimane Prefecture, {{ill|Unnan, Shimane|en}}[[ja:雲南市]], Kamo Town || || || Mirror inscribed "Jingchu Third Year" |- | {{ill|Takeshima Go-Karō Residence Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:竹島御家老屋敷古墳]] || Yamaguchi Prefecture, {{ill|Shūnan, Yamaguchi|en}}[[ja:周南市]] || || || Mirror inscribed "Zhengshi First Year" |- ! colspan=5| Shikoku Region |- | {{ill|Oku No. 3 Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:奥三号墳]] || Kagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en}}[[ja:さぬき市]], Samukawa Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Nishiyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:西山古墳]] || Kagawa Prefecture, Sanuki, Kamobe Nishiyama || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Miyatani Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:宮谷古墳]] || Tokushima Prefecture, {{ill|Tokushima|en}}[[ja:徳島市]], Kokufu Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd to early 4th century || |- ! colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市]], Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:石塚山古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:京都郡]], {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:苅田町]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:糸島市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en}}[[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], Usa Fudoki Hill || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:川部・高森古墳群]]; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市]], Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:石塚山古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:京都郡]], {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:苅田町]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:糸島市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en}}[[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], Usa Fudoki Hill || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:川部・高森古墳群]]; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市]], Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:石塚山古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:京都郡]], {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:苅田町]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:糸島市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en}}[[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], Usa Fudoki Hill || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:川部・高森古墳群]]; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市]], Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:石塚山古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:京都郡]], {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:苅田町]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:糸島市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en}}[[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], Usa Fudoki Hill || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:川部・高森古墳群]]; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] to [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- ! colspan=5| |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | {{ill|Shiratori Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白鳥古墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en}}[[ja:東広島市]], Takaya Town || || || |- | {{ill|Shiozaki Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:潮崎山古墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuyama|en}}[[ja:福山市]], Shinichi Town || || || |- | {{ill|Nakakoda No. 1 Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:中小田1号墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Hiroshima|en}}[[ja:広島市]], Higashi Ward || || || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound |- | {{ill|Kambara Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神原神社古墳]] || Shimane Prefecture, {{ill|Unnan, Shimane|en}}[[ja:雲南市]], Kamo Town || || || Mirror inscribed "Jingchu Third Year" |- | {{ill|Takeshima Go-Karō Residence Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:竹島御家老屋敷古墳]] || Yamaguchi Prefecture, {{ill|Shūnan, Yamaguchi|en}}[[ja:周南市]] || || || Mirror inscribed "Zhengshi First Year" |- ! colspan=5| Shikoku Region |- | {{ill|Oku No. 3 Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:奥三号墳]] || Kagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en}}[[ja:さぬき市]], Samukawa Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Nishiyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:西山古墳]] || Kagawa Prefecture, Sanuki, Kamobe Nishiyama || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Miyatani Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:宮谷古墳]] || Tokushima Prefecture, {{ill|Tokushima|en}}[[ja:徳島市]], Kokufu Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd to early 4th century || |- ! colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市]], Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:石塚山古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:京都郡]], {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:苅田町]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:糸島市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en}}[[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], Usa Fudoki Hill || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:川部・高森古墳群]]; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- ! colspan=5| |- | || || || || |- | || || || || |- | {{ill|Shiratori Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白鳥古墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en}}[[ja:東広島市]], Takaya Town || || || |- | {{ill|Shiozaki Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:潮崎山古墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuyama|en}}[[ja:福山市]], Shinichi Town || || || |- | {{ill|Nakakoda No. 1 Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:中小田1号墳]] || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Hiroshima|en}}[[ja:広島市]], Higashi Ward || || || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound |- | {{ill|Kambara Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神原神社古墳]] || Shimane Prefecture, {{ill|Unnan, Shimane|en}}[[ja:雲南市]], Kamo Town || || || Mirror inscribed "Jingchu Third Year" |- | {{ill|Takeshima Go-Karō Residence Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:竹島御家老屋敷古墳]] || Yamaguchi Prefecture, {{ill|Shūnan, Yamaguchi|en}}[[ja:周南市]] || || || Mirror inscribed "Zhengshi First Year" |- ! colspan=5| Shikoku Region |- | {{ill|Oku No. 3 Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:奥三号墳]] || Kagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en}}[[ja:さぬき市]], Samukawa Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Nishiyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:西山古墳]] || Kagawa Prefecture, Sanuki, Kamobe Nishiyama || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Miyatani Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:宮谷古墳]] || Tokushima Prefecture, {{ill|Tokushima|en}}[[ja:徳島市]], Kokufu Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd to early 4th century || |- ! colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市]], Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:石塚山古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:京都郡]], {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:苅田町]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:糸島市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en}}[[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], Usa Fudoki Hill || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:川部・高森古墳群]]; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Shiratori Burial Mound]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)
## Related Topics * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)

revision.text:
'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)
## Related Topics * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)
## Related Topics * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)}}

(しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in **Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in **Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture**. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in **Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in **Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture**. (しらとりこふん)

(しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

revision.text:
'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several burial mounds (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)
## Related Topics * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

revision.text:
'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)

revision.text:
'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} - Located in '''Moriyama-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふんぐん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Located in '''Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture'''. (しろとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)}} - Located in '''Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん) * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)}} - Located in '''Hirao, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture'''. (しらとりこふん)
## Related Topics * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)}}

revision.text:
'''白鳥古墳'''（しらとりこふん、しろとりこふん）は、日本の古墳の名称。
* [[白鳥古墳群]] - 愛知県名古屋市守山区。しらとりこふんぐん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] - 愛知県名古屋市熱田区。しろとりこふん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (東広島市)]] - 広島県東広島市。しらとりこふん。 * [[白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] - 山口県熊毛郡平生町。しらとりこふん。
== 関連項目 == * [[白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)]]
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の古墳|しらとりこふん]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11580542]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11580542]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11580542]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白鳥塚古墳}}

* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|Atsuta Ward, Nagoya]], Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Higashihiroshima|Higashihiroshima]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao]], Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580542]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en|Higashihiroshima|WD=Q653372|lt=Higashihiroshima}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Hirao|en|Hirao|WD=Q1205194|lt=Hirao}}, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白鳥塚古墳}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥古墳]]
'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en|Higashihiroshima|WD=Q653372|lt=Higashihiroshima}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Hirao|en|Hirao|WD=Q1205194|lt=Hirao}}, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白鳥塚古墳}}

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en|Higashihiroshima|WD=Q653372|lt=Higashihiroshima|1=Higashihiroshima}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Hirao|en|Hirao|WD=Q1205194|lt=Hirao|1=Hirao}}, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白鳥塚古墳}}

'''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳, Shiratori Kofun) is the name of several Kofuns (kofun) in Japan.
* {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun-gun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shirotori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja|白鳥古墳 (東広島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Higashihiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en|Higashihiroshima|WD=Q653372|lt=Higashihiroshima|1=Higashihiroshima}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.'' * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Hirao|en|Hirao|WD=Q1205194|lt=Hirao|1=Hirao}}, Kumage District, Yamaguchi Prefecture. Pronounced ''Shiratori Kofun.''
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白鳥塚古墳}}

Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Kofuns in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Kofuns |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Kofuns. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Kofuns |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |}
